summary: A collection of praise, thanksgiving, and urgent lament — from harvest hymns and a missionary blessing to the deeply Messianic cry of Psalm 69, whose suffering and reproaches are fulfilled in Christ.
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
65Hymn / ThanksgivingDavidHarvest thanksgiving, possibly after drought reliefGod's forgiveness and his lavish provision for all creationv.3 “When iniquities prevail against me, you atone for our transgressions”v.2 (“to you all flesh shall come”) anticipates the universal reach of the Gospel; Christ is the fulfillment of God's atoning provision
66Hymn / Individual ThanksgivingUnknownAfter national and personal deliverance; vows being fulfilled at the templeGod's testing leads to abundance; his mighty deeds in history and in individual livesv.10 “For you, O God, have tested us; you have tried us as silver is tried”The refining imagery anticipates 1 Pet 1:7; the pattern of suffering leading to abundance is fulfilled in Christ's death and resurrection
67Hymn / Missionary PsalmUnknownHarvest blessing; prayer for God's blessing to extend to all nationsGod's saving way known among all nations; Israel blessed to be a blessingv.2 “that your way may be known on earth, your saving power among all nations”Anticipates the Great Commission (Matt 28:18-20); the Aaronic blessing (Num 6:24-26) fulfilled universally through the Gospel
69Individual Lament / MessianicDavidSevere persecution; suffering for righteousness; enemies without causeRighteous suffering borne for God's sake; zeal for God's house; plea for vindicationv.9 “Zeal for your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen on me”Most-quoted OT psalm in the NT: v.4 (John 15:25), v.9 (John 2:17; Rom 15:3), v.21 (Matt 27:34,48; John 19:29), v.22-23 (Rom 11:9-10), v.25 (Acts 1:20)
70Individual Lament / PetitionDavidUrgent plea for immediate deliverance from enemies; nearly identical to Ps 40:13-17Urgency in calling on God; the Lord as immediate help and delivererv.5 “But I am poor and needy; hasten to me, O God! You are my help and my deliverer; O LORD, do not delay!”The urgent cry “make haste” reflects the believer's dependence on Christ as the only deliverer; the “poor and needy” posture is fulfilled in the beatitudes (Matt 5:3)
